panels.title: P50 Latency
panels.description: Median request latency across all services. This is the latency most users experience under normal conditions.

panels.title: P95 Latency
panels.description: 95th-percentile latency. Rising values indicate that the slowest 5% of requests are getting worse, often the first sign of saturation.

panels.title: P99 Latency
panels.description: 99th-percentile latency. This captures worst-case tail latency; sustained increases may indicate resource contention or GC pauses.

panels.title: Total Spans
panels.description: Total number of spans analyzed in the selected time window. Provides context for the statistical significance of the percentile values.

panels.title: P95 Latency by Service
panels.description: Plots 95th-percentile latency over time grouped by service. Diverging lines help isolate which service is the source of a latency regression.

panels.title: Latency Distribution
panels.description: Shows the distribution of request durations across latency buckets. A long right tail indicates outlier requests that may disproportionately affect user experience.

panels.title: Latency Heatmap
panels.description: Visualizes request latency over time with color intensity representing request density at each duration band. Hot bands shifting upward over time indicate progressive latency degradation.

panels.title: Latency Budget by Service
panels.description: Shows total time spent in each service. Services consuming disproportionate latency budget are prime candidates for optimization.

panels.title: Top Slowest Operations (p99)
panels.description: Lists operations ranked by 99th-percentile duration, showing the worst tail-latency offenders. Focus optimization efforts on the top entries for maximum user-impact improvement.

panels.title: Top Error Operations
panels.description: Lists operations ranked by error rate. High error rates often correlate with latency spikes due to retries and fallback logic.
